summary.intent: Fix the broken boot-md hook (GitHub #594) by migrating BOOT.md from a no-op hook-based approach to the established workspace markdown pattern used by SOUL.md, AGENTS.md, TOOLS.md, and MEMORY.md.
summary.outcome: Implementation completed across all 7 target files: new loader functions added to config crate, boot_text threaded through PromptPersona and prompt-building functions, the broken boot_md.rs hook deleted, hook registration removed from server.rs, and docs updated. Tests delegated to a subagent.

repo: Workspace markdown files (SOUL.md, AGENTS.md, TOOLS.md, MEMORY.md, now BOOT.md) all follow a single pattern: loaded per-session via PromptPersona, injected via build_system_prompt_full()
GatewayStart is a read-only event that fires once at server start, not per session — hook-based injection at this event is fundamentally wrong for per-session context
The boot_message key returned by ModifyPayload had zero consumers in the codebase — the feature was silently broken from the start
builtin_hook_metadata() in crates/gateway/src/server.rs maintains a list of built-in hook descriptions that must be kept in sync with actual registered hooks
append_truncated_text_block() + WORKSPACE_FILE_MAX_CHARS are the standard utilities for injecting workspace files into the system prompt with size limits
load_boot_md_for_agent() follows agent-local-first-then-global fallback pattern consistent with other workspace file loaders

code.finding: build_system_prompt_with_session_runtime and build_system_prompt_minimal_runtime both needed a new boot_text: Option<&str> parameter; every test call site also required updating — there were many inline and multi-line call patterns
Multiple call sites with three different indentation levels required separate replace_all passes for persona.soul_text.as_deref() -> boot_text insertion
New public functions must be explicitly re-exported from crate root lib.rs, not just defined in loader.rs

workflow: When a parameter is added to a widely-called function, the number of test call sites can be unexpectedly large — delegating that mechanical update to a subagent is effective
replace_all on multi-argument function call patterns requires careful handling of indentation variations across call sites
Reading files in parallel at session start (all 7 target files simultaneously) significantly reduces time before edits begin

friction: Three different indentation levels for the same call pattern in chat/src/lib.rs required three separate replace_all passes instead of one
Test call sites in prompt.rs were numerous and mixed inline/multi-line formats, requiring delegation to a subagent to avoid tedious one-by-one edits
build_system_prompt convenience wrapper parameter count had to be manually verified after adding the new boot_text param to ensure None counts stayed correct
open_items: Verification commands not yet run: cargo test -p moltis-agents -- boot, cargo test -p moltis-config -- boot, cargo check --workspace, just lint, just format-check
Test additions (test_boot_text_injected_into_prompt and test_load_boot_md_for_agent) were delegated to a subagent — outcome not confirmed in transcript
docs/src/hooks.md edit added BOOT.md to workspace context section — should verify the section exists and placement is correct relative to other workspace files
